Date: 2024-02-20
Duration: 45 minutes

[00:02:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on territory planning from our last session.

[00:08:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:21:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:25:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:31:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:36:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-B trap (Blockers miscategorized (technical labeled commercial or vice versa)); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-B failure mode (Blockers miscategorized (technical labeled commercial or vice versa))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-B -- Blockers miscategorized (technical labeled commercial or vice versa)
